summary: sensivity@2.5.0 installs Windows autostart persistence and exfiltrates hardware fingerprints via an obfuscated payload
details: On require/load, launcher.js shells out to PowerShell to write an HKCU Run-key entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' that points wscript.exe at a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which silently re-launches `node launcher.js` at every login. The launcher also sets `process.title = 'Runtime Broker'` to impersonate a legitimate Windows process. launcher.js then `eval()`s a 110KB obfuscator.io-packed sibling, server.obf.js, which reads HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid via PowerShell and the C: volume serial via `cmd /c vol C:`, combines them with the hostname into an HWID, and POSTs `{key, hwid, nonce, app, version}` to a hardcoded license endpoint constructed via string concatenation as `...opi.com/check.php`. After the license check, the obfuscated server loads an opaque 6.8MB native addon `sens.node` (no source, no binding.gyp) and calls `forte.start()/forte.set()`; the bundled UI exposes aimbot/triggerbot/ESP controls. The combination of autostart persistence under a Microsoft-impersonating name, process-name spoofing, eval of a heavily obfuscated payload, hardware-fingerprint exfiltration to an author-controlled endpoint, and an opaque privileged native binary auto-loaded after persistence is established constitutes an active attack on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
